title: USD Namespace Source Capability
purpose: Define the reusable boundary consumed by stage population. A namespace source supplies composed or degenerate-composed prim/property opinions by path without requiring the stage-population skill to know whether the data came from one layer, sublayers, references, payloads, variants, or usd-composition-arbitrator machinery.

included: absolute root path as the root scene object
enumeration of candidate child prim names for a prim path
enumeration of candidate property names for a prim path
typed field opinion lookup for prims and properties
strength-ordered contributing opinions for downstream composition providers
defaultPrim and layer-level population field access
provider declaration for raw or composed child/property ordering
schema-defined property injection when supplied by a schema-aware namespace source
instance-filtered descendant inputs when supplied by a composed namespace source that declares instancing partial or full
deferred: composition arc evaluation unless supplied by usd-composition-arbitrator or another composed namespace source
value resolution beyond the strongest or only opinion exposed by the namespace source
schema fallback property injection unless supplied by a schema-aware namespace source
payload load policy beyond reporting whether the source supports it
complete scene graph instancing unless a composed namespace source declares instancing full

model.NamespaceSource: Target-native provider consumed by usd-stage-population. It exposes scene-object inputs by path and hides whether those inputs come from a single layer or a composed namespace.
model.NamespaceSourceMode: Capability descriptor such as single_layer, layer_stack, composed_namespace, or full_composition.
model.SceneObjectInput: The provider's path-addressed input for one prim-like scene object. It can expose field opinions, child names, property names, and source diagnostics.
model.FieldOpinion: A target-native typed field value exposed to stage population. For a single-layer provider this is the authored field value; for usd-composition-arbitrator this is the composed strongest field or a value-resolution-ready opinion stack. It preserves stored type identity and checked typed-read semantics from the document-model/value contracts.
model.ContributingOpinion: A strength-ordered typed provenance record containing a source identifier and semantic spec path, never a composite source:path string in domain storage.
model.CapabilityLevel: Structured declaration of whether composition, value resolution, payload load state, instancing, schema fallbacks, and population masks are complete, partial, or absent.
model.OrderingDeclaration: Structured declaration of whether childNames and propertyNames are raw source lists that require consumer ordering or already composition-ordered lists that consumers must preserve.

input.path: absolute scene prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.exists: whether the source has a scene-object input at this path
output.fields: field opinions visible for this path
output.childNames: candidate child prim names before stage mask/active filtering, either raw or composed according to source_capabilities.child_ordering
output.propertyNames: candidate property names before schema fallback injection, either raw or composed according to source_capabilities.property_ordering

output.exists: whether the source has an attribute or relationship input at this path
output.fields: field opinions visible for this property

input.path: absolute scene prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak contributing scene-object opinions with origin, composition arc class, spec path, fields, child names, and property names sufficient for later composition providers

output.diagnostics: diagnostics produced by the source before population

laws: namespace-source paths are path handles or equivalent target-native path references, not parser-local strings

long-lived namespace-source records that carry source paths, target paths, mapped paths, variant-selection paths, child names, property names, variant set names, or selected variant names must follow the semantic-runtime-types contract
field names are token/field-token identity values, not parser-local string keys
field opinions preserve target-native typed value identity and checked typed reads; adapter JSON may be materialized only at conformance or serialization boundaries
a namespace source must declare its capability level so stage population does not silently assume full composition
stage population may consume ordered child and property names from the namespace source, but must own population-mask filtering and active-descendant pruning

field maps alone are not sufficient when a downstream composition provider must insert opinions at a specific LIVERPS strength position; providers that feed later composition providers must expose strength-ordered contributing opinions or an equivalent target-native opinion stack

strength-ordered contributing opinions must use typed composition provenance records; adapter strings such as source.usda:/Prim must be rendered only at diagnostics, reports, ABI, or conformance boundaries
a raw_single_layer child/property ordering declaration means stage population applies single-layer primOrder/propertyOrder rules over raw source child/property lists

a composed child/property ordering declaration means the provider has already applied composition ordering and stage population must preserve the order while applying only population-mask and active filtering
composition providers may query source-visible variant-selection paths validated by usd-paths, but stage population must receive only composed scene paths
a single-layer namespace source must not perform sublayer, reference, payload, inherits, specializes, variant, relocates, value-clip, or schema fallback evaluation

usd-composition-arbitrator or another composed namespace source may provide the same capability with stronger composition and value-resolution guarantees without changing the stage population contract
a schema-aware namespace source may provide schema-defined property names and schema fallback fields without requiring stage population to compute prim definitions
adapter JSON may seed or inspect a namespace source in conformance tests, but domain APIs must exchange target-native layer, path, token, and field value objects
